vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1614: Substrate: deacon does not recover a fully-stopped review convoy on an OPEN in-review issue

plan.title: Substrate: deacon does not recover a fully-stopped review convoy on an OPEN in-review issue
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The deacon's checkOrphanedReviewStatuses re-dispatches a 'pending' review only when status.prUrl is set AND the work agent's completed.processed marker exists (deacon.ts:1569-1639). When an OPEN, in-review issue's entire review convoy stops (work agent + synthesis + all four sub-role reviewers all 'stopped', with no pause/troubled gate) but that precondition is unmet, the issue silently wedges: the pending path never re-dispatches, never increments reviewRetryCount, never trips the PAN-794 breaker, and never escalates. PAN-1242 rotted in in-review for 9 days with no live agent. This is the same class of bug as PAN-1247 — a recovery gate with no recovery path is an infinite stall, and under autonomous Flywheel runs no human is watching individual convoys.
narratives.Proposal: Add a dedicated, bounded deacon patrol recoverStalledReviewConvoys() modeled exactly on recoverUnhealthyTestStack (cooldown + attempt cap + escalate-once). It treats 'canonical state = in_review (OPEN) AND reviewStatus in-flight (reviewing|pending) AND every convoy agent stopped AND no pause/troubled gate AND not stuck/deaconIgnored' as a recoverable orphan, and force-re-dispatches the review convoy via spawnReviewRoleForIssue({...,force:true}). A per-issue in-memory state map bounds retries; on cap exhaustion it escalates once (activity-log error + mark review status stuck with a dedicated reason) so it stops looping and surfaces in the dashboard. Wired into runPatrol after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses. Delivered as three beads: (1) a convoy-liveness helper, (2) the bounded recovery patrol with unit tests, (3) wiring into the patrol cycle.

autoDecisions.summary: New function recoverStalledReviewConvoys() instead of extending checkOrphanedReviewStatuses.
autoDecisions.rationale: checkOrphanedReviewStatuses is already ~370 dense lines (1367-1735); a separate function is independently testable and matches the codebase precedent of bounded self-heal living in its own helper (recoverUnhealthyTestStack).

autoDecisions.summary: Detection requires canonical state === 'in_review' specifically, not merely 'open'.
autoDecisions.rationale: verifying_on_main and in_progress are also OPEN; acting on them would re-dispatch reviews that should not run. The issue is explicitly about in-review stalls.

The patrol has already confirmed via state.json that the convoy is fully stopped/stale; force guarantees a clean fresh convoy against current HEAD and force-killing already-dead sessions is harmless (killAllReviewerSessions catches errors).

Matches TEST_STACK_REBUILD_COOLDOWN_MS / TEST_STACK_REBUILD_MAX_ATTEMPTS and the PAN-794 REVIEW_INFRA_BREAKER_THRESHOLD already used in this file, keeping recovery cadence consistent across the deacon.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState() (cached 10 min) for the OPEN check, only for issues whose reviewStatus is already in-flight.
autoDecisions.rationale: Avoids adding uncached gh calls to every patrol cycle; bounds API usage to a tiny candidate set.

autoDecisions.summary: On cap exhaustion, mark the review status stuck and emit one activity-log error.
autoDecisions.rationale: A bounded recovery must end in a visible, non-looping terminal — silent give-up is the exact failure PAN-1614 reports. stuck=true makes both this patrol and checkOrphanedReviewStatuses skip the issue until a human unsticks.

items.title: Add reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId) helper enumerating work + synthesis + sub-role agents

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts add a helper (e.g. reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId): { anyLive: boolean; anyGated: boolean; agentIds: string[] }) that enumerates the review convoy agents for an issue: the work agent `agent-<id>`, the synthesis agent `agent-<id>-review`, and one `agent-<id>-review-<subRole>` for each subRole in REVIEW_SUB_ROLES (imported from review-monitor.js). For each, read state via getAgentStateSync. anyLive is true if any agent's status is 'running' or 'starting'. anyGated is true if any agent has paused===true or troubled===true. Absent state.json (null) counts as stopped and ungated. Keep it pure/synchronous (no spawning, no status mutation).

subItems.title: Helper enumerates exactly six agent IDs: agent-<id>, agent-<id>-review, and agent-<id>-review-<subRole> for each of security, correctness, performance, requirements (sourced from REVIEW_SUB_ROLES, not hardcoded literals).
anyLive === true iff at least one enumerated agent has state.json status 'running' or 'starting'; a null/absent state.json is treated as stopped.
anyGated === true iff at least one enumerated agent has paused===true OR troubled===true.
Helper performs no side effects (no spawnRun, no setReviewStatus, no tmux kills) — confirmed by a unit test that asserts no mocked mutators are called.

Unit tests cover: all stopped → anyLive=false/anyGated=false; one sub-role running → anyLive=true; work agent paused → anyGated=true; a sub-role troubled → anyGated=true; all state.json absent → anyLive=false/anyGated=false. npm run typecheck, lint, and test pass.

items.title: Add bounded recoverStalledReviewConvoys() patrol (cooldown + attempt cap + escalate-once)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts add and export async function recoverStalledReviewConvoys(): Promise<string[]>, modeled structurally on recoverUnhealthyTestStack (deacon.ts:1291-1365). Iterate loadReviewStatuses(); for each issue: skip unless reviewStatus is in-flight ('reviewing' or 'pending'); skip if status.stuck or status.deaconIgnored. For the surviving candidates, resolve the canonical tracker state via getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState(issueId) and require it to equal 'in_review'. Call reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId); require anyLive===false AND anyGated===false. For a matching stalled convoy, apply a per-issue in-memory recovery-state Map<string,{lastAttempt:number;attempts:number;escalated:boolean}> with a 15-minute cooldown and a cap of 3 attempts: (a) if attempts>=cap, escalate once (emitActivityEntrySync level:'error' with a 'stalled-review-convoy-unrecoverable' message AND setReviewStatusSync stuck:true with a dedicated stuckReason e.g. 'review_convoy_unrecoverable'), then return; (b) if within cooldown, defer this cycle; (c) otherwise increment attempts, set lastAttempt, resolve workspace via resolveProjectFromIssueSync + findWorkspacePath (skip without consuming an attempt if unresolvable), and force-re-dispatch via Effect.runPromise(spawnReviewRoleForIssue({ issueId, workspace, branch: `feature/<lower>`, force: true })). On a successful dispatch, delete the issue's map entry. Push a descriptive string to the actions array for every branch (re-dispatched / deferred-cooldown / escalated / skipped-unresolvable). Wrap the body in try/catch like checkOrphanedReviewStatuses so one bad issue never aborts the whole patrol.

subItems.title: Re-dispatches the convoy (spawnReviewRoleForIssue with force:true) exactly when canonical state==='in_review' AND reviewStatus in {'reviewing','pending'} AND reviewConvoyLiveness reports anyLive=false AND anyGated=false AND not stuck AND not deaconIgnored.

Does NOT re-dispatch when any convoy agent is live (anyLive=true), when any agent is paused/troubled (anyGated=true), when reviewStatus is terminal (passed/failed/blocked), when status.stuck or status.deaconIgnored is true, or when canonical state is anything other than 'in_review' (e.g. verifying_on_main, in_progress, done, canceled, null).
Within the 15-minute cooldown after an attempt, a subsequent call defers (no second spawnReviewRoleForIssue call) and records a 'deferring — cooldown' action.

After the attempt cap (3) is reached, the patrol escalates exactly once: emits one emitActivityEntrySync error AND calls setReviewStatusSync with stuck:true and a dedicated stuckReason; subsequent calls neither re-dispatch nor re-escalate (escalated flag prevents duplicate activity entries).
A successful re-dispatch clears the issue's entry from the in-memory recovery-state map so a future independent stall gets a fresh attempt budget.
When the workspace/project cannot be resolved, the patrol records a skip action and does NOT consume an attempt from the cap.

getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState is only consulted for issues whose reviewStatus is already in-flight (candidate set filtered first), and a thrown error from any single issue is caught so the patrol returns its accumulated actions without aborting.

Unit tests in tests/lib/cloister/ cover the full matrix in ac1-ac6 (recover, each skip reason, cooldown defer, cap exhaustion+escalation+stuck, successful-dispatch map clear, unresolvable-workspace skip), mocking review-status, agents (getAgentStateSync/spawn), spawnReviewRoleForIssue, and getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState. npm run typecheck, lint, and test pass.

items.title: Wire recoverStalledReviewConvoys() into runPatrol after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses

narrative.Action: In runPatrol() (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts), call recoverStalledReviewConvoys() immediately after the checkOrphanedReviewStatuses() block (around deacon.ts:4341-4344). Push its returned actions into the patrol actions array and addLog each one with state.patrolCycle, following the exact pattern used by the surrounding patrol calls (e.g. orphanActions / missingStatusActions).

subItems.title: recoverStalledReviewConvoys() is invoked once per patrol cycle, after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses() and after recoverOrphanedAgents().
Returned actions are appended to the patrol's actions array and each is passed to addLog('action', a, state.patrolCycle), matching the surrounding patrol wiring.
npm run typecheck, lint, and the full test suite pass; the deacon patrol cycle still completes without regressions.
